title: Valle Verde: A Voice for the Valley

success_criteria.description: All five districts are in use and every precinct is assigned.
All five districts have roughly equal population (within 10%).
At least one district must have a Latino population of 50% or more — giving the Valley community a fair opportunity to elect their preferred representative.
All districts are reasonably compact — the Valley district hugs the Valley, not a long tendril reaching for favorable precincts (compactness ≥ 35%).

character.name: You
character.role: Court-Appointed Redistricting Coordinator, Valle Verde County
character.motivation: A federal court found that the current district map violates the Voting Rights Act. The Valley's Latino community has been cracked across multiple districts — diluted so thoroughly that they cannot elect their preferred candidate anywhere. You must draw a new map that complies with the law.

intro_slides.heading: The Valley Grows
intro_slides.body:
Over the past decade, Valle Verde County's Valley has seen significant population growth. The Latino community, concentrated in this area, now makes up a meaningful share of the county's total population.

But on the current map, drawn ten years ago, the Valley is sliced across multiple districts — each one a piece of a different district, none large enough to matter.

intro_slides.heading: The Voting Rights Act
intro_slides.body:
Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act prohibits maps that dilute minority voting power. When a minority community is sufficiently large, geographically compact, and politically cohesive, mapmakers must give them a fair opportunity to elect their preferred representative.

The Valley meets all three conditions. A court has already ruled that the current map is illegal. Your job is to fix it.

intro_slides.heading: A Tradeoff You'll See
intro_slides.body:
Creating a majority-Latino district solves the legal problem — but notice what happens to the surrounding districts. Concentrating a cohesive voting bloc into one place can shift the balance everywhere else.

This is one of the real tensions in redistricting. Even compliance with a fairness law reshapes who wins and loses. There is no map without consequences.

narrative.objective: Draw at least one majority-Latino district (≥ 50% Latino population) to give the Valley community a fair chance to elect their preferred representative.
